subject: A Review of Set Theory
question: What is the total number of outcomes of tossing a fair coin n times.
explanation: If S is the sample space then $S = S_1 \times S_2 \times \ldots \times S_n$ where $S_i$, $1 \le i \le n$, is the set consisting of the two outcomes H = head and T = tail. By the previous theorem, $n(S) = 2^n$
